title: QuickStart
code: # retrieve data from TENxVisium eh = ExperimentHub::ExperimentHub() q = AnnotationHub::query(eh, 'TENxVisium') id = q$ah_id[q$title=='HumanGlioblastoma_v3.13'] spe = eh[[id]]

title: QuickStart
code: # 1. prepare CleanSlide() inputs coord.xy = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe) %>% as.data.frame() ncount = colSums(assay(spe))  # 2. run CleanSlide() res = CleanSlide(coord = coord.xy, nCount = ncount)  # 3. store results in SpatialExperiment object colData(spe) = cbind(colData(spe), res)  # 4. apply filter automatically determined by threshold calculation spe = spe[,spe$threshold]

title: SpotGraphs
text: demonstrate general usage SpotGraph, use 10X Visium dataset squamous cell carcinoma patient, downloaded GSE208253. raw data GEO provided Seurat object package use example.
code: class(scc_s1) #> [1] "Seurat" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SeuratObject" dim(scc_s1) #> Loading required namespace: SeuratObject #> [1] 36601  1185

title: SpotGraphs
text: goal package support Visium analysis providing function constructs igraph object based number immediately adjacent spots slide. opens analysis pipeline various igraph functions perform certain tasks : identifying spots lying tissue debris removed downstream analysis distance calculations spots measuring shortest path network spot easily identifying edges pre-defined regions tissue construct igraph object, need x,y coordinates spots slide. can plot igraph object ggnetwork package, interfaces directly ggplot2, use SpatialPlotGraph function provided package visualize edges drawn node original x,y coordinates stored igraph object. Note is_boundary attribute automatically calculated running SpotGraph(), use color node plots.
code: coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')] head(coord) #>                        x     y #> AAACACCAATAACTGC-1 16571  4809 #> AAACAGGGTCTATATT-1 13546  3944 #> AAACCGTTCGTCCAGG-1 14812  8142 #> AAACGAGACGGTTGAT-1 10536 13505 #> AAACTGCTGGCTCCAA-1 13053 11764 #> AAAGACTGGGCGCTTT-1  9011  4240  ig = SpotGraph(coord = coord) ig #> IGRAPH 18e39df UNW- 1185 3189 --  #> + attr: name (v/c), coord_x (v/n), coord_y (v/n), is_boundary (v/l), #> | weight (e/n) #> + edges from 18e39df (vertex names): #>  [1] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--AGGCGGTTTGTCCCGC-1 #>  [2] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--CTCGTCGAGGGCTCAT-1 #>  [3] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--GAAACATAGGAAACAG-1 #>  [4] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--GGAACCTTGACTCTGC-1 #>  [5] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--TCCCTGGCGTATTAAC-1 #>  [6] AAACACCAATAACTGC-1--TGGACGCAATCCAGCC-1 #>  [7] AAACAGGGTCTATATT-1--ACAGTAATACAACTTG-1 #> + ... omitted several edges plt.ggnet = ggplot(ig, aes(x=x, y=y, xend=xend, yend=yend)) +   geom_edges() +    geom_nodes(aes(color = is_boundary), size = 0.5) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = 'none')  plt.spg = SpatialPlotGraph(igraph_object = ig,                             group.by = 'is_boundary',                             flip.axes = T,                             pt.size = 0.5)  patchwork::wrap_plots(plt.ggnet, plt.spg)

title: SpotGraphs
text: One main features SpotGraph package identify spots slide lie top tissue debris disconnected rest tissue sample. uninformative technical artifact sample processing, ’d like exclude downstream analysis. CleanSlide() function provides simple way automatically identify spots, based connectivity spots number transcripts detected within community spots. First, extract two inputs required CleanSlide() function: x,y coordinates spot dataset vector transcript counts detected spot output CleanSlide() data.frame number rows input coordinate data.frame matrix can directly used Seurat::AddMetaData() re-insert back original Seurat object. can now observe results CleanSlide() Seurat::SpatialDimPlot()  can agree results directly apply threshold filter spots subset(scc_s1, threshold), can manually identify groups spots want filter . ‘ig_cluster’ metadata column stores communities spots, spot immediately adjacent spots. case, manually remove clusters 9, 10, 23, 24, 26, 22, 15, 18, 19, 17, 20, 25.
code: coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')] nCounts = scc_s1$nCount_Spatial res = CleanSlide(coord, nCount = nCounts) scc_s1 = Seurat::AddMetaData(scc_s1, res) Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(scc_s1, group.by = 'threshold', image.alpha = 0.6) Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(scc_s1, group.by = 'ig_cluster', image.alpha = 0.6, label = T, label.size = 3) spot_clusters_to_remove = c(9, 10, 23, 24, 26, 22, 15, 18, 19, 17, 20, 25) scc_s1 = scc_s1[, !scc_s1$ig_cluster %in% spot_clusters_to_remove] Seurat::SpatialFeaturePlot(scc_s1, features = 'nCount_Spatial', pt.size.factor = 2)

title: SpotGraphs
text: working igraph object, may desirable remove edges two groups spots.
code: # Create igraph object coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = coord[,c('x', 'y')] ig = SpotGraph(coord)  # Clustering with igraph igraph::V(ig)$clusters = factor(igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(ig)$membership) plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'clusters', label = T) +   ggtitle('before') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none')  # Remove edges between pairs of clusters ig = CutEdges(ig, cluster_pairs = list(c(2,1), c(2,10)), cluster.col = 'clusters') plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'clusters', label = T) +   ggtitle('after') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none')  plt3 = plt2 +    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.35,0.8), ylim = c(0.4, 1)) +   ggtitle('after/zoomed') patchwork::wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3)

what: Load several datasets to get a survey of potential cluster_nCount distributions
title: ThresholdTests
code: # retrieve data from TENxVisium eh = ExperimentHub::ExperimentHub() q = AnnotationHub::query(eh, 'TENxVisium') ds.names = c('HumanLymphNode',               'MouseBrainCoronal',              'HumanColorectalCancer_v3.13',              'HumanSpinalCord_v3.13',              'HumanGlioblastoma_v3.13',              'MouseKidneyCoronal_v3.13') id = q$ah_id[q$title %in% ds.names] spe.ls = lapply(id, function(ds.id) eh[[ds.id]]) names(spe.ls) = ds.names %>% str_replace('\\.', '_')

title: ThresholdTests
code: spe.ls = lapply(spe.ls, function(spe) {   colData(spe)$nCount = colSums(assay(spe))   return(spe) }) res.ls = lapply(spe.ls, function(spe) {   coord = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(spe) %>% as.data.frame()   colnames(coord) = c('x', 'y')   CleanSlide(coord = coord, nCount = spe$nCount) })

what: Store the result dataframe in each SpatialExperiment object
title: ThresholdTests
code: for (ds.name in names(res.ls)) {   colData(spe.ls[[ds.name]]) = cbind(colData(spe.ls[[ds.name]]), res.ls[[ds.name]]) }

title: ThresholdTests
code: den = density(log10(cluster_nCount.ls$HumanLymphNode)) df = data.frame(x=den$x, y=den$y) ggplot(df, aes(x=x, y=y)) +   geom_line() +   theme_classic() ImageFeature = function(obj, feature = 'nCount', label = F) {   # First, scale the x,y coordinates to fit the low-res H&E image   lowres_scale <- imgData(obj)[imgData(obj)$image_id == 'lowres', 'scaleFactor']   coord.xy = SpatialExperiment::spatialCoords(obj) %>% as.data.frame()   coord.xy$x_axis <- coord.xy$pxl_row_in_fullres * lowres_scale   coord.xy$y_axis <- coord.xy$pxl_col_in_fullres * lowres_scale        # Flip the y-axis   coord.xy$y_axis <- abs(coord.xy$y_axis - (ncol(imgRaster(obj)) + 1))        # Get metadata feature from object   coord.xy[,feature] = colData(obj)[,feature]    # Create plot of transcripts per spot overlaid ontop of H&E   plt = ggplot(mapping = aes(1:600, 1:600)) +     annotation_raster(imgRaster(obj),                        xmin = 1, xmax = 600,                        ymin = 1, ymax = 600) +     geom_point(data=coord.xy, alpha=0.5, size = 0.5,                aes(x=x_axis, y=y_axis, colour=get(feature))) +     scale_color_viridis(option = 'turbo', name = feature) +     coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1,600),                      ylim = c(1,600)) +     coord_fixed() +     theme_void()      if (label) {     df.labels = coord.xy %>%        reframe(.by = feature,                x_axis = mean(x_axis),               y_axis = mean(y_axis))          plt = plt +       geom_label(data = df.labels, aes(x=x_axis, y=y_axis, label = get(feature)))   }   return(plt) }  tmp = ImageFeature(spe.ls[[1]], feature = 'cluster_nCount', label = T) plt.ncount = lapply(names(spe.ls), function(ds.name) {   ImageFeature(spe.ls[[ds.name]], feature = 'cluster_nCount', label = T) +     ggtitle(ds.name) +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold')) }) wrap_plots(plt.ncount, ncol = 3)

title: ThresholdTests
code: cluster_nCount.ls = lapply(res.ls, function(res) {   unique(res$cluster_nCount) })

what: Write the current helper function to identify the threshold
title: ThresholdTests
code: # helper to calculate threshold, given a vector of transcript counts get_threshold = function(cluster_nCount) {   # log the total counts per cluster to smooth out the density   den = density(log10(cluster_nCount))    # define an interval to check for local minima   int.min = quantile(den$x, 0.05) #previous value of 0 might be to low   int.max = quantile(den$x, 0.75) #maybe 3rd quantile?   interval = c(int.min, int.max)    # calculate local minima   thres = approxfun(den$x, den$y) %>% optimise(interval=interval)   thres = 10^(thres$minimum)   return(thres) }

title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: create igraph object given 2-column data frame matrix x,y coordinates node = spot, edge exists two spots adjacent returns igraph object takes 2-column data frame matrix x,y coordinates calls SpotGraph create igraph object performs modularity maximization group connected communities spots adds total transcripts together per-cluster (nCount) calculates threshold identify low quality/small communities spots returns data frame per-spot cluster results, total counts per cluster, whether cluster passed automatically detected threshold use original x,y coordinates plot vertex_attr corresponding igraph object remove edges specified groups spots igraph object

what: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
text: Identify spots 10X Visium Seurat object potentially low quality. Mostly useful slides large areas without tissue standard spot-filtering based nCounts insufficient. Identify neighboring spots x,y coordinates SpotGraph() Performing modularity maximization identify clusters Calculate total number transcripts detected cluster (nCount) Set threshold identify clusters filtered

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
code: CleanSlide(coord, nCount)

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. nCount named vector transcript counts per spot, names spot barcodes/ids, element match row coord.

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
text: data.frame following columns: ig_cluster: igraph modularity maximization results cluster_nCount: summed transcript counts within 'ig_cluster' group threshold: whether respective 'ig_cluster' group passed automatically determiend threshold barcode: spot id barcode sequence

title: Identify spots isolated from the largest groups of immediately adjacent spots — CleanSlide
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame with two isolated groups of points df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y') nspots = dim(df)[1] rownames(df) = paste0('spot', 1:nspots)  # Create some vector of transcript counts spotcounts = rbinom(25, 10000, 0.3) spotcounts = c(spotcounts, rbinom(9, 100, 0.3)) names(spotcounts) = rownames(df)  # The resulting data.frame should indicate that the smaller # cluster of 3x3 points did not pass the threshold, while the # larger group of 5x5 points should have passed the threshold. res = CleanSlide(coord = df, nCount = spotcounts) } # }

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
code: CutEdges(igraph_object, cluster_pairs = NULL, cluster.col = "cluster")

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
text: igraph_object igraph object cluster results stored. cluster_pairs list containing two-element vectors indicating pairs cluster names want remove edges . cluster.col character value vertex attribute name igraph object clustering results.

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
text: updated igraph object edges removed

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y')  # Create an igraph object with these coordinates ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Perform clustering and add those cluster assignments to the igraph object cl = igraph::cluster_louvain(ig)$membership ig = igraph::set_vertex_attr(ig, 'cluster', value = factor(cl))  # Examine graph before removing edges SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'cluster')  # Remove edges between clusters 1 and 2, and between clusters 3 and 4 ig = CutEdges(igraph_object = ig,               cluster_pairs = list(c(1,2), c(1,3)),               cluster.col = 'cluster')  # Examine graph after removing edges SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'cluster') } # }

title: Visualize igraph vertex attribute on tissue x,y coordinates — SpatialPlotGraph
text: igraph_object igraph object coord (optional) two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. provided, look coord_x coord_y attributes provided igraph object. group.vertex attribute plot onto tissue coordinates. Must present vertex_attr(ig). label Logical, whether label groups plot based group.. flip.axes Default TRUE. Invert x y axes, typically needed coordinates Visium Seurat objects align spots tissue image. pt.size Numeric input determine size point plot. Used size parameter geom_point().

code: SpotGraph(coord, dist.buffer = 1.05, max.dist = NULL)

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. dist.buffer Influences maximum distance node allowed another node considered neighbor. used max.dist = NULL. max.dist furthest node another node considered neighbors. NULL (default), shortest distance two nodes minimum distance (min.dist) required two nodes considered neighbors, max.dist calculated hypotenuse triangle two sides length min.dist, multiplied dist.buffer.

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: igraph object, vertex (.e., node) corresponds row coord input, un-weighted edges vertices immediately adjacent .

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame with two isolated groups of points df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y')  # Preview the points on a grid with their corresponding x,y coordinates ggplot(df, aes(x = x,y=y)) +   geom_point()  # Identify points that are immediately adjacent to each other and create an igraph object ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Optionally view the network with ggnetwork # library(ggnetwork) ggplot(ig, aes(x=x, y=y, xend=xend, yend=yend)) +   geom_edges() +   geom_nodes() } # }
